name: metacognitive-guard
description: Monitors Claude's responses for struggle signals and suggests escalation to deep-thinking agents when complexity exceeds comfortable reasoning capacity.
capabilities: Monitor responses for struggle signals like hedging, deflection, and contradictions
Provide a self-assessment checklist before responding to complex questions
Suggest proactive escalation to deep-thinking agents based on complexity indicators
Guide users on crafting effective escalation prompts with context and constraints
Help identify high-complexity situations requiring multi-domain synthesis
Track and improve personal struggle detection awareness over time
author: ANcpLua
securitySummary: All 18 static findings are false positives. Scanner misinterpreted markdown code formatting (backticks) as command execution, GitHub URLs as network endpoints, and metacognitive terms as security threats. This is a benign metacognitive guidance skill with no actual security risks.
valueStatement: Claude sometimes struggles with highly complex problems that require deep analysis. This skill monitors your responses for struggle signals and recommends proactive escalation to deep-thinking agents before quality degrades.
userTitle: Escalate Complex Problems to Deep-Thinking Agents
limitations: Cannot automatically trigger agent escalation without user permission
Does not access external tools or files for analysis
Struggle detection is based on pattern recognition, not perfect understanding
Cannot replace human judgment on when escalation is truly necessary

useCases.title: Architectural Decision Support
useCases.description: When facing complex system design decisions with competing constraints across performance, maintainability, and cost dimensions, escalate to deep-thinking agents for structured analysis.

useCases.title: Multi-Domain Problem Solving
useCases.description: When problems span multiple technology areas without obvious integration patterns, use the skill to recognize the need for specialized deep analysis.

useCases.targetUser: Users new to Claude and AI assistants
useCases.title: Quality Response Calibration
useCases.description: New Claude users learning to recognize when their responses lack confidence or clarity can use this skill to improve response quality through self-assessment.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Struggle Check
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm about to respond to a user question. Help me check if I should escalate: Can I give a concrete recommendation with high confidence? Is this answerable without multiple follow-ups? Would structured analysis help?

promptTemplates.title: Complexity Evaluation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this problem for escalation indicators: Does it involve architectural decisions with competing constraints? Are requirements ambiguous? Does it require multi-domain synthesis? Does it need edge case analysis?

promptTemplates.title: Crafting Escalation Prompt
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create an effective escalation prompt for the deep-think-partner agent. Include: system context, specific constraints, success criteria, and the core question needing analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Post-Escalation Synthesis
promptTemplates.prompt: The deep-think-partner returned analysis on [topic]. Help me synthesize the key insight, present a clear recommendation, and offer an implementation plan for the user.

bestPractices: Use the self-assessment checklist before responding to any complex question - answer honestly about your confidence level.
Escalate proactively when you detect ambiguity in requirements or multiple valid architectural approaches.
After receiving deep-thinking analysis, synthesize the key insight rather than pasting the full response.
antiPatterns: Responding with 'it depends' without identifying the specific constraints that matter.
Continuing to struggle through a complex problem without considering escalation.
Pasting deep-thinking partner output verbatim without synthesizing for the user.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically escalate to other agents?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and recommendations but requires you to trigger escalation through the Task tool. You maintain full control over when to engage deep-thinking agents.

faq.question: How does struggle detection work?
faq.answer: The skill identifies patterns like hedging language, deflection, contradictions, verbose responses without concrete output, and non-committal recommendations. Use the self-assessment checklist to catch these early.

faq.question: What makes a problem complex enough to escalate?
faq.answer: Indicators include architectural decisions with competing constraints, ambiguous requirements, multi-domain synthesis needs, and edge case analysis requirements. The skill provides specific checklists for each category.

faq.question: Can I use this with other thinking or reasoning skills?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill complements other reasoning tools by helping you recognize when deeper analysis would improve response quality. It does not replace structured thinking approaches.

faq.question: How accurate is the struggle detector?
faq.answer: The detector relies on pattern recognition in your own responses. It may produce false positives or miss genuine struggles. Track your triggers over time to calibrate your personal patterns.

faq.question: What should I include in an escalation prompt?
faq.answer: Include context about the system or codebase, specific constraints limiting the solution, clear success criteria, and the specific question or decision needing analysis. The skill provides templates for each element.
